Ex. Ord. No. 14314. Making America Beautiful Again by Improving Our National ParksEx. Ord. No. 14314, , 90 F.R. 30201, provided:July 3, 2025By the authority vested in me as President by the Constitution and the laws of the United States of America, it is hereby ordered:1. . From the awe-inspiring Grand Canyon to the tranquility of the Great Smoky Mountains, America’s national parks have provided generations of American families with unforgettable memories. It is the policy of my Administration to preserve these opportunities for American families in future generations by increasing entry fees for foreign tourists, improving affordability for United States residents, and expanding opportunities to enjoy America’s splendid national treasures.SectionPolicy2. . (a) The Secretary of the Interior shall develop a strategy to increase revenue and improve the recreational experience at national parks by appropriately increasing entrance fees and recreation pass fees for nonresidents in areas of the National Park System that charge entrance fees or recreation pass fees as defined in []. Additionally, to the extent consistent with applicable law, the Secretary of the Interior, working with the Secretary of Agriculture as appropriate, shall take steps to increase the prices at which the America the Beautiful Pass—the National Parks and Federal Recreational Lands Pass—and any site-specific agency or regional multi-entity passes are sold to nonresidents.Sec.Making America Beautiful Again by Improving Our National Parks16 U.S.C. 6801section 802 of Pub. L. 108–447(b) The Secretary of the Interior shall use any increased fee revenue generated pursuant to subsection (a) of this section to improve the infrastructure of, or otherwise enhance enjoyment of or access to, America’s Federal recreational areas, consistent with [].16 U.S.C. 6807section 808 of Pub. L. 108–447(c) The Secretary of the Interior, working with the Secretary of Agriculture as appropriate, shall take steps to improve services and affordability for United States residents visiting national parks, as consistent with applicable law.(d) The Secretary of the Interior, working with the Secretary of State, shall work to encourage international tourism to America’s national parks and outdoor recreation areas, and especially wider utilization of America’s many such areas that may be underutilized.(e) The Secretary of the Interior shall review the maintenance backlog within the National Park Service and take all appropriate action to fully implement the National Parks and Public Land Legacy Restoration Fund established in the Great American Outdoors Act () [see Tables for classification], invest in the infrastructure of national parks, and increase visitor capacity to allow more Americans to visit national parks.Public Law 116–152(f) The Secretary of the Interior shall review all of the Department of the Interior’s rules and policies related to public use of national parks and take actions consistent with applicable law to ensure that the National Park Service manages national parks consistent with the policy of this order. The Secretary of the Interior shall review all of the Department’s recreational access rules and take steps to rescind any that unnecessarily restrict recreation in national parks. In conducting this review, the Secretary of the Interior shall especially scrutinize all recreational access rules or other restrictions promulgated or enacted during the prior administration. The Secretary of the Interior shall take appropriate actions to grant American residents preferential treatment with respect to any remaining recreational access rules, including permitting or lottery rules, consistent with applicable law.3. . The Presidential Memorandum of (Promoting Diversity and Inclusion in Our National Parks, National Forests, and Other Public Lands and Waters) [ note], is hereby revoked.Sec.RevocationJanuary 12, 201742 U.S.C. 2000e4. . (a) Nothing in this order shall be construed to impair or otherwise affect:Sec.General Provisions(i) the authority granted by law to an executive department, agency, or the head thereof; or(ii) the functions of the Director of the Office of Management and Budget relating to budgetary, administrative, or legislative proposals.(b) This order shall be implemented consistent with applicable law and subject to the availability of appropriations.(c) This order is not intended to, and does not, create any right or benefit, substantive or procedural, enforceable at law or in equity by any party against the United States, its departments, agencies, or entities, its officers, employees, or agents, or any other person.(d) The costs for publication of this order shall be borne by the Department of the Interior.Donald J. Trump.

children.heading: Entrance fee
children.label: 1 Entrance fee
children.text: The term “entrance fee” means the recreation fee authorized to be charged to enter onto lands managed by the National Park Service or the United States Fish and Wildlife Service.

children.heading: Expanded amenity recreation fee
children.label: 2 Expanded amenity recreation fee
children.text: The term “expanded amenity recreation fee” means the recreation fee authorized by .section 6802(g) of this title

children.heading: Federal land management agency
children.label: 3 Federal land management agency
children.text: The term “Federal land management agency” means the National Park Service, the United States Fish and Wildlife Service, the Bureau of Land Management, the Bureau of Reclamation, or the Forest Service.

children.heading: Federal recreational lands and waters
children.label: 4 Federal recreational lands and waters
children.text: The term “Federal recreational lands and waters” means lands or waters managed by a Federal land management agency.

children.heading: National Parks and Federal Recreational Lands Pass
children.label: 5 National Parks and Federal Recreational Lands Pass
children.text: The term “National Parks and Federal Recreational Lands Pass” means the interagency national pass authorized by .section 6804 of this title

children.heading: Passholder
children.label: 6 Passholder
children.text: The term “passholder” means the person who is issued a recreation pass.

children.heading: Recreation fee
children.label: 7 Recreation fee
children.text: The term “recreation fee” means an entrance fee, standard amenity recreation fee, expanded amenity recreation fee, or special recreation permit fee.

children.heading: Recreation pass
children.label: 8 Recreation pass
children.text: The term “recreation pass” means the National Parks and Federal Recreational Lands Pass or one of the other recreation passes available as authorized by .section 6804 of this title

children.heading: Recreation service provider
children.label: 9 Recreation service provider
children.text: The term “recreation service provider” means a person that provides recreational services to the public under a special recreation permit under clause (iii) or (iv) of paragraph (13)(A).

children.heading: Secretaries
children.label: 10 Secretaries
children.text: The term “Secretaries” means the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Agriculture acting jointly.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary of the Interior, with respect to a Federal land management agency (other than the Forest Service); and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary of Agriculture, with respect to the Forest Service.

children.heading: Special account
children.label: 12 Special account
children.text: The term “special account” means the special account established in the Treasury under for a Federal land management agency.section 6806 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: an organizational camp;

children.label: II
children.text: a single event that does not require an entry or participation fee that is not strictly a sharing of expenses for the purposes of the event; and

children.label: III
children.text: participation by the public in a recreation activity or recreation use of a specific area of Federal recreational lands and waters in which use by the public is allocated;

children.label: ii
children.text: for a large-group activity or event of 75 participants or more;

children.label: aa
children.text: is a structured or scheduled event or activity;

children.label: bb
children.text: is not competitive and is for fewer than 75 participants;

children.label: cc
children.text: may charge an entry or participation fee;

children.label: dd
children.text: involves fewer than 200 visitor-use days; and

children.label: ee
children.text: is undertaken or provided by the recreation service provider at the same site not more frequently than 3 times a year;

children.label: II
children.text: a single competitive event; or

children.label: aa
children.text: is a structured or scheduled activity;

children.label: bb
children.text: is not competitive;

children.label: cc
children.text: may charge a participation fee;

children.label: dd
children.text: occurs in a group size of fewer than 7 participants;

children.label: ee
children.text: involves fewer than 40 visitor-use days; and

children.label: ff
children.text: is undertaken or provided by the recreation service provider for a term of not more than 180 days; or

children.label: I
children.text: a recurring outfitting, guiding, or, at the discretion of the Secretary, other recreation service, the authorization for which is for a term of not more than 10 years; or

children.label: II
children.text: a recurring outfitting, guiding, or, at the discretion of the Secretary, other recreation service, that occurs under a temporary special recreation permit authorized under .section 8545 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: a concession contract for the provision of accommodations, facilities, or services;

children.label: ii
children.text: a commercial use authorization issued under ; orsection 101925 of title 54

children.label: iii
children.text: any other type of permit, including a special use permit administered by the National Park Service.

children.heading: Special recreation permit fee
children.label: 14 Special recreation permit fee
children.text: The term “special recreation permit fee” means the fee authorized by .section 6802(h)(2) of this title

children.heading: Standard amenity recreation fee
children.label: 15 Standard amenity recreation fee
children.text: The term “standard amenity recreation fee” means the recreation fee authorized by .section 6802(f) of this title

children.heading: State
children.label: 16 State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the several States, the District of Columbia, and each territory of the United States.
